entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal grain crop with global production of 59 million t in 2003

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The yield per unit area, averaging 1.34 t ha-1 globally for sorghum

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The process of developing improved cultivars to increase productivity

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Region where research stations recorded sorghum productivity of 3.5-4.0 t ha-1

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country in Asia where research stations recorded sorghum productivity of 3.5-4.0 t ha-1

entities.name: MYANMAR
entities.description: Country in Asia where research stations recorded sorghum productivity of 3.5-4.0 t ha-1

entities.name: PAKISTAN
entities.description: Country in Asia where research stations recorded sorghum productivity of 3.5-4.0 t ha-1

entities.name: THAILAND
entities.description: Country in Asia where research stations recorded sorghum productivity of 3.5-4.0 t ha-1

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: Continent containing India, Myanmar, Pakistan, and Thailand

entities.name: CHINA
entities.description: Country where research station productivity is 12 t ha-1 and farmer field productivity is 0.8-2.5 t ha-1

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY GAP
entities.description: The difference between potential productivity at research stations and actual productivity in farmers' fields

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Biological factors contributing to the productivity gap

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Non-biological factors contributing to the productivity gap

relationships.description: Sorghum has a measured global average productivity
Breeding is used to increase productivity
Productivity was recorded at research stations in African countries
Productivity was recorded at research stations in India
Productivity was recorded at research stations in Myanmar
Productivity was recorded at research stations in Pakistan
Productivity was recorded at research stations in Thailand
Productivity was recorded at research stations in Asian countries
Productivity was recorded at research stations and farmers' fields in China
Biotic stresses account for the productivity gap
Abiotic stresses account for the productivity gap
The productivity gap is the difference in productivity levels
